title: Pass 5.G.2: ferrotorch-data WeightedRandomSampler O(N²) + Fisher-Yates modulo bias
description:
Pass 4 finding (umbrella #982) — ferrotorch-data audit, PERF_OBVIOUS.

- src/sampler.rs:266-286 — WeightedRandomSampler without-replacement rebuilds the cumulative-weights vector from scratch for every draw (O(N²) for the sampler instead of O(N) reservoir or Walker's alias). With num_samples = N, this is quadratic in dataset size on the hot epoch path.
- src/sampler.rs:24-30 — shuffle_with_seed uses `state as usize % (i+1)` Fisher-Yates, which has modulo bias for non-power-of-two i+1. Cheap fix (rejection sampling).

Acceptance:
- Walker's alias method for WeightedRandomSampler without-replacement (O(N) preprocess, O(1) per draw)
- Rejection sampling for Fisher-Yates index
- Distributional test: 10⁶ draws should pass chi-square against expected weights at 95%.
